herb_constituents.name: chrysophanol
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis chrysophanol, also known as chrysophanic acid, is a lignan compound found in the roots of the Angelica sinensis plant. This compound has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Research has shown that chrysophanol has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a potential therapeutic agent for various diseases.

herb_constituents.name: chrysophanic acid
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis chrysophanic acid is a lignan compound with reported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Research suggests that it may have potential therapeutic applications in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, although further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety. The specific mechanisms of action and potential health benefits of chrysophanic acid require further investigation and clinical trials.

herb_constituents.name: quercetin
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis quercetin, a flavonoid compound, has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its anti-inflammatory properties. Research suggests that quercetin may help to reduce oxidative stress and alleviate symptoms associated with various diseases, including cardiovascular and neurodegenerative disorders. Its potential therapeutic benefits have led to increased interest in studying quercetin as a valuable component of Angelica sinensis.

herb_constituents.name: phthalides
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis phthalides have been studied for their potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The compounds are also thought to have cardiovascular benefits, such as lowering blood pressure and improving lipid profiles. However, further research is needed to fully understand the effects and potential applications of phthalides in human medicine.

herb_constituents.name: β-sitosterol
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis β-sitosterol is a triterpenoid saponin compound found in the medicinal plant Angelica sinensis, commonly known as Dong quai. It has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, contributing to its potential health benefits. Additionally, β-sitosterol has been shown to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health by reducing cholesterol levels and preventing the formation of blood clots.

herb_constituents.name: oleanolic acid
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis oleanolic acid has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential benefits in treating various health conditions. Studies have suggested that oleanolic acid may also exhibit anti-cancer and anti-viral activities, further highlighting its medicinal value. Additionally, oleanolic acid has been reported to have protective effects on the liver and cardiovascular system, making it a promising compound for the prevention and treatment of various diseases.

herb_constituents.name: friedelin
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis friedelin has been found to possess various biological activities, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It has been reported to exhibit potential in protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation, which may contribute to its medicinal applications. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects of friedelin and its potential uses in treating various diseases.

herb_constituents.name: kaempferol
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis kaempferol is a bioactive compound that has been isolated from the root of Angelica sinensis, a traditional Chinese medicinal herb. Studies have shown that kaempferol possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Additionally, kaempferol has been found to have neuroprotective and cardiovascular protective effects, making it a promising area of research in the field of pharmacology.

herb_constituents.name: luteolin
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis luteolin, a flavonoid compound, has been found to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Studies have also suggested that luteolin may have protective effects against cardiovascular disease by reducing inflammation and improving blood lipid profiles. Additionally, luteolin has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, potentially aiding in the prevention or treatment of neurodegenerative diseases.

herb_constituents.name: guaiol
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis guaiol, a bioactive sesquiterpene, has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Studies have shown that guaiol may exert these effects through the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and the scavenging of free radicals. Further research is needed to fully elucidate the therapeutic potential of guaiol in the treatment of various diseases.

herb_constituents.name: triterpenoid saponins
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis triterpenoid saponins have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties and are believed to contribute to the plant's cardiovascular and cerebrovascular protective effects. These compounds have also been shown to exhibit antioxidant activity, which may help to mitigate oxidative stress and cell damage. Studies suggest that triterpenoid saponins from Angelica sinensis may have therapeutic potential in the treatment of various diseases, including atherosclerosis and neurodegenerative disorders.

herb_constituents.name: ursolic acid
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis ursolic acid has been reported to possess various pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer properties. Research has demonstrated its ability to modulate cellular signaling pathways and inhibit the growth of cancer cells. Additionally, studies have suggested that ursolic acid may have neuroprotective effects and contribute to the prevention of neurodegenerative diseases.

herb_constituents.name: isorhapontigenin
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis isorhapontigenin has been identified as a bioactive compound that possesses antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties. Research suggests that isorhapontigenin may have potential therapeutic benefits in the treatment of neurodegenerative diseases, such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Additionally, studies have also shown that isorhapontigenin may exhibit anti-cancer properties, inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis.

herb_constituents.name: isoorientin
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis isoorientin is a medicinal active constituent of the traditional Chinese medicinal herb Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai. It has been reported to possess various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties. Research suggests that isoorientin may contribute to the herb's traditional uses in treating menopausal symptoms and other gynecological disorders.

herb_constituents.name: apigenin
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis apigenin is a flavonoid compound found in the traditional Chinese medicinal herb Angelica sinensis, commonly known as dong quai. It has been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating menstrual disorders and menopausal symptoms. Apigenin has also been shown to exhibit anti-cancer and neuroprotective effects in various in vitro and in vivo studies.

herb_constituents.name: triterpenoids
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis triterpenoids are bioactive compounds found in the plant Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. Studies have shown that these triterpenoids possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, making them a promising area of research for potential therapeutic applications. Further studies are needed to fully understand the mechanisms and effects of triterpenoids in the body.

herb_constituents.name: phthalide glycosides
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis phthalide glycosides have been found to have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties, which contribute to their potential therapeutic effects in treating various diseases. Studies have shown that these compounds can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reduce oxidative stress, leading to improved cardiovascular and cerebrovascular health. The anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative activities of phthalide glycosides may also be beneficial in managing conditions such as atherosclerosis and Alzheimer's disease.

herb_constituents.name: quercetin-3-o-β-d-glucoside
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis quercetin-3-o-β-d-glucoside is a bioactive compound that has been found to exhibit potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Research suggests that this constituent may contribute to the medicinal effects of Angelica sinensis in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. Further studies are needed to fully understand the mechanisms and benefits of quercetin-3-o-β-d-glucoside in traditional medicine.

herb_constituents.name: nortriterpenoids
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis nortriterpenoids are a class of bioactive compounds found in Angelica sinensis, a traditional Chinese medicinal herb. Studies have shown that nortriterpenoids possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to their potential health benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand the pharmacological effects of nortriterpenoids and their role in traditional Chinese medicine.

herb_constituents.name: eudesmanolides
herb_constituents.desc: Angelica sinensis eudesmanolides are a class of sesquiterpene lactones found in Angelica sinensis, a traditional Chinese medicinal plant. Research suggests that eudesmanolides may possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to their potential health benefits. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the effects of eudesmanolides on human health.
